title: Toolchain Selection, Compatibility, and Reproducible Setup · Gate Forensics
summary: Select a minimal compatible toolchain and prove clean-machine setup. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a pinned bootstrap and compatibility matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin supported runtime and package-manager versions with one authoritative source.
Commit appropriate lockfiles and verify deterministic clean installs.
Choose tools with clear ownership and minimal duplicate diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-bootstrap-smoke-19u65u: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Bootstrap Smoke into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-bootstrap-smoke-19u65u:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Bootstrap Smoke precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates toolchain. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: If setup works on the author machine it is documented correctly.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Bootstrap Smoke applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test setup, build, and checks from a clean environment.
